Pax diarahkan agar claim melalui website Thai Airways

1. VJ848 DPS-SGN 30JAN Pax who sit at OWE row cannot understand the instructions from cabin crew about the Overwing Exit.
 2. Loading position not match with LDM/Load Summary
During our busy day on date 20Feb25 handling pax ex AOG VJ998/19Feb25. We find out no ramp staff duty for flight VJ893/20Feb25.
PAX CANCEL ACCEPT, ACTUAL ONBOARD & MISSING HANDCOUNT

1. The sudden gate change and flight delay forced staff to redirect passengers, leading to operational stress
 and miscommunication.
 2. The presence of large family groups and excess hand luggage caused distractions, reducing staff focus on
 verifying individual passengers.
 3. The inexperience of newly assigned staff and their unfamiliarity with e-boarding procedures contributed to
 procedural errors and miscommunication.
 4. The lack of attentiveness by the handcount staff resulted in an inaccurate final passenger count.

1. - Implement a real-time coordination system between the airline, ground handling, and airport authorities to
 anticipate gate changes and communicate them promptly to all staff.
 - Introduce pre-planned alternative gate assignments to minimize last-minute operational stress.
 - Ensure clear passenger announcements and deploy dedicated staff to efficiently redirect passengers.
 2. - Deploy additional gate staff during peak hours or when flights with large family groups are scheduled.
 - Implement a strict two-piece carry-on baggage policy enforcement at check-in to prevent delays at the
 boarding gate.
 - Utilize boarding sequence enforcement tools (e.g., digital queue displays) to reduce congestion and
 distractions for staff.
 3. Conduct intensive refresher training for all newly assigned staff, focusing on:
 - E-boarding pass verification
 - Passenger profiling
 - Handling boarding system alerts
 - Immigration and travel document checks
 - Assign an on-the-job mentor for newly assigned staff to guide them during live operations.
 - Implement a mandatory knowledge validation test before assigning staff to critical gate positions.
 4. - Introduce a strict no-phone policy during critical boarding phases to maintain full staff attention.
 - Utilize two-person verification for manual handcount, ensuring redundancy in counting procedures

 check CCTV to ensure during unloading, towing and break down the bge in normal condition.

2. Seluruh Flight yg beroperasi di malam hari akan dilakukan timbang ulang di make upa area tanpa terkecuali yg berindikasi terjadinya manipulasi berat bagasi , khususnya tujuan DJJ, MKW, MKQ, SOQ, TTE, AMQ, MDC, UPG
The check-in counter staff did not follow the procedure on flight VN630 on 19 February 2025.
Pelanggaran Regulasi bisa mengarah pada denda atau sanksi
Segera memverifikasi kesalahan atau ketidaksesuaian prosedur pada proses check-in, Memberikan penjelasan yang jelas dan sopan kepada Airlines mengenai kesalahan yang terjadi

Stricter supervision is required for the operation of the Conveyor Belt Loader (CBL), particularly regarding the speed and ensuring that the Side Hand Rail is properly installed during unloading and loading activities.
Root cause analysis and corrective actions taken, as well as follow-up for CCTV footage review.
Several safety issues during aircraft landing and handling, including the lack of Ramp personnel, improper equipment handling, and failure to communicate requests.
Findings from the IOSA 2024 audit highlight the need for improved safety measures, with specific follow-up actions required.
Manipulasi/penukaran label bagasi oleh oknum protokol Februari 2025

Indikasi manipulasi berat bagasi dengan menukar label bagasi dengan yang lain yang berbeda berat dan fisiknya, yang dilakukan oleh Protokol pada saat bagasi dibawa ke OOG, menyebabkan perbedaan signifikan antara berat di label dan berat aktual.

Melakukan hard briefing kepada seluruh tim check-in guna memastikan kepatuhan terhadap prosedur penanganan bagasi serta mencegah terjadinya manipulasi berat bagasi maupun penyimpangan terkait transaksi keuangan (money matter).
